  "decision": "fail",

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Condenser/heat pump pad is not level, preventing proper oil return to the compressor.",
    "Non-level installation violates manufacturer installation instructions and IMC/IRC M1401 requirements for secure and level mounting."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down unit to prevent compressor damage.",
    "Re-level pad to within manufacturer's specified slope tolerance (typically ±1/4 inch per foot or as specified).",
    "Verify level after correction and check for proper oil return before restarting."
  ],
  "rationale": "A non-level condenser/heat pump can cause oil to pool in the condenser coil rather than returning to the compressor, leading to inadequate lubrication, overheating, and premature compressor failure. Manufacturer instructions and IMC/IRC M1401 require equipment to be installed level and secure. The observed condition is a clear defect that poses a high risk of equipment damage and must be corrected before the system is placed in service.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer installation manual (leveling requirements)",
    "IMC 304.1 / IRC M1401.3 (Equipment installation, leveling, and support)"
  ]
}
